canonical_invariant: If no valid object exists -> nothing happens.

exact_object_discipline: validated_object_hash must be produced by /compile before /validate returns VALID
/execute must require a VALID validation row matching decision_id, session_id, validated_object_hash, and invocation_nonce
/proof must require execution_id, decision_id, validated_object_hash, session_id, and workflow/run/commit lineage
Any mutation after validation must produce NULL/hash_mismatch before execution
fail_closed_behavior: Missing API key, DB binding, route, schema, authority, validation, execution, or proof lineage returns NULL or an explicit blocked status
Proposal/review workflows may generate artifacts only; they must not call /execute or /proof
Legacy/demo objects have no implicit authority
replay_resistance: invocation_nonce is single use for one decision_id and validated_object_hash
authority cannot be reused after RESERVED/EXECUTED/CONSUMED transitions
proof uniqueness must block duplicate proof for the same decision_id and validated_object_hash
continuity lineage reuse or revocation cascades to authority/validation/invocation state
proof_persistence: Every successful execution must be followed by proof persistence before authority becomes CONSUMED
Proof must persist session_id, continuity_id, continuity_hash, identity_id, execution_id, decision_id, validated_object_hash, workflow, environment, run_id, and commit_sha
Proof lineage must equal execution lineage
non_bypassability: No direct production deploy outside governed-deploy.yml
No direct production DB mutation outside governed migration process
No webhook mutation may execute from static demo objects
No hidden legacy entrypoint may become production-capable
continuity_lineage: session_id, identity_id, continuity_id, and continuity_hash must bind authority, validation, execution, and proof
Revoked or expired continuity returns NULL
Orphaned execution without active continuity returns NULL

exact_object.given: A VALID object hash exists for decision_id + invocation_nonce
exact_object.when: execute receives a different validated_object_hash or mutated target/scope
exact_object.then: return NULL with hash_mismatch/no_validation and do not insert execution/proof
return NULL/no_validation and do not mutate execution_registry

exact_object.when: /proof receives mismatched decision_id, session_id, execution_id, or validated_object_hash
exact_object.then: return NULL/lineage_mismatch and do not consume authority

replay_resistance.when: /execute is called again with the same nonce
validate or execute attempts to reuse the authority
/proof is called again for same object
replay_resistance.then: return NULL or archive duplicate without changing canonical proof

continuity_lineage_scaffolding.then: return NULL and cascade revocation to dependent rows

continuity_lineage_scaffolding.when: stored continuity_hash differs from rederived hash

continuity_lineage_scaffolding.then: proof contains continuity_id, continuity_hash, identity_id, authority_lineage, and execution_lineage

runtime_integrity_findings.finding: Production deploy command is not directly observed; governed-deploy currently proves runtime authorization but does not contain a direct wrangler deploy step. Preserve this unless a future deploy step is inserted strictly after /execute and before /proof with exact object binding.
D1 migrations and local migration scripts are raw state mutation surfaces and need separate production migration governance before production apply.
Legacy/demo files remain present; disabled entrypoints reduce risk, but registry.js/compile-decision.js/static demo objects must remain quarantined from production authority.
Manual workflow dispatch remains available but is bounded by exact decision_id, validated_object_hash, invocation_nonce, worker API key, and runtime replay checks.

unresolved_risks: Credential/secret boundary cannot be fully verified from repository contents alone.
Cloudflare production D1 migration permissions cannot be verified from local files alone.
GitHub branch protection and environment protection settings cannot be fully verified from repository contents alone.
Legacy/demo scripts are present and require continued quarantine discipline.
